artifacts.content:
From: Carla J. Mendoza <c.mendoza4827@gmail.com>
To: Financial Counseling Office <finassist@rochesterregional.org>
Date: 2025-02-11 09:42 AM
Subject: Verification of Income for Financial Assistance Application

Dear Financial Counseling Team,

I’m writing to clarify a couple of items on my financial assistance application for my oncology and lymphedema therapy bills. I completed the online form last night but realized I may not have explained our situation clearly, especially around my husband’s income and my small craft sales.

My husband, Daniel Mendoza, is a senior network technician with Rochester Regional Health. His W-2 for 2024 shows gross earnings of $67,250 before taxes and deductions. In addition, I earned approximately $1,250 last year from my small crochet business (“Willow Ridge Threads”) before expenses. Together, that puts our total earned annual gross household income, income before taxes and deductions, at 68500.0 for 2024.

I’ve attached Daniel’s W-2, our most recent pay stubs, and a simple spreadsheet I use to track my craft sales. I’m not currently employed otherwise; I had to leave my third-grade teaching position when I started breast cancer treatment in late 2022.

Please let me know if you also need copies of our 2024 tax return, recent medical bills, or pharmacy receipts. The co-pays for chemo, imaging, and now ongoing physical therapy are adding up quickly, and I want to be sure my application is complete so there are no delays.

You can reach me by email or at my cell, 585-555-2934, if anything is unclear or if you need additional documentation.

Thank you for your help and for taking the time to review our case.

Sincerely,

Carla J. Mendoza
4827 Willow Ridge Drive
Rochester, NY 14618

metadata.subject: Verification of Income for Financial Assistance Application

artifacts.content:
From: billing@rrhfinancialservices.org
To: carlamendoza4827@gmail.com
Date: 2025-01-09 08:17 AM
Subject: Income Verification Needed for Financial Assistance Renewal

Dear Ms. Mendoza,

Thank you for submitting your renewal application for the Rochester Regional Health Patient Financial Assistance Program.

To complete our review, we need confirmation of your household’s **gross annual income before taxes and deductions**. On your application you listed your **estimated 2024 household income as $72,000** based primarily on your husband’s wages from Rochester Regional Health, along with a small amount of side income from craft sales. However, the most recent pay stubs we received for Daniel Mendoza (senior network technician) suggest a slightly different figure.

Please provide one of the following within 14 days:

1. A copy of your 2024 federal tax return (Form 1040), including all schedules, OR
2. Copies of Daniel’s last 3 pay stubs showing year-to-date gross earnings, OR
3. A written statement explaining why the **projected gross household income of $72,000** differs from the year-to-date totals shown on his pay statements.

If your crocheting business “Willow Ridge Threads” generated more than **$1,500 in gross receipts** last year, please also include a simple income/expense summary so we can correctly calculate your pre-tax household income.

Failure to provide the requested documentation may delay or reduce your assistance level.

Sincerely,

Karen Wells
Financial Counseling Specialist
Rochester Regional Health
(585) 555-0142

This message may contain confidential information and is intended only for the recipient named above.

metadata.subject: Income Verification Needed for Financial Assistance Renewal

artifacts.content:
From: assistance@monroecounty.gov
To: carlamendoza4827@gmail.com
Date: 2024-10-03 3:44 PM
Subject: Preliminary Eligibility Estimate – Monroe County Prescription Cost Relief

Dear Carla Mendoza,

We have reviewed the preliminary information you submitted through the online screening tool for the Monroe County Prescription Cost Relief Program.

Based on the figures you entered, your **estimated gross household income before taxes and deductions is $59,400 per year**, consisting of your spouse’s salary and overtime as a senior network technician at Rochester Regional Health and a small amount of self-employment income from your crochet sales.

This estimated **$59,400** places your family at approximately 215% of the current Federal Poverty Level for a four-person household. Under current program guidelines, households between 200% and 250% of FPL may qualify for partial assistance with prescription co-pays, subject to verification of:

• Most recent federal tax return
• Last 30 days of pay stubs for all working adults
• Documentation of any self-employment or gig income

Please note that this is only a **screening estimate**, not an official determination. Your final eligibility may change once we review your actual income documentation and consider medical expenses related to your breast cancer treatment.

If the **$59,400 gross income** figure you provided is not accurate, please update your information before your scheduled phone interview on October 17.

Sincerely,

Monroe County Human Services
Benefits Screening Unit
Phone: (585) 555-2290

metadata.subject: Preliminary Eligibility Estimate – Monroe County Prescription Cost Relief

Attendees: Carla, NY State of Health rep ("Melissa")

Notes:

Call-in from kitchen so binder is handy. Have the following ready:

Melissa said they mainly care about:

3. Out-of-pocket medical costs from chemo, PT, and imaging – might help with cost-sharing reductions.

Questions to ask:

artifacts.content:
From: info@brightonfoodcupboard.org
To: carlamendoza4827@gmail.com
Date: 2024-11-20 09:02 AM
Subject: Documentation Needed for Holiday Assistance Application

Dear Carla,

Thank you for reaching out to the Brighton Food Cupboard regarding our 2024 Holiday Assistance Program. We’re glad to be able to support families in the Brighton school district who are facing increased financial strain due to medical or employment changes.

On your intake form, you indicated a **current gross household income of approximately $52,800 per year** from your husband’s employment, noting that you are not currently working due to ongoing breast cancer treatment. You also mentioned a small amount of seasonal income from selling crocheted hats and scarves at local craft fairs.

To finalize your application, please email or bring copies of the following:

1. Proof of **$52,800 annual gross income** (recent pay stub showing year-to-date earnings or your latest tax return)
2. Documentation of any additional income over $100/month (e.g., Venmo or PayPal summaries for your crochet sales)
3. Recent medical or pharmacy bills that show how treatment costs are impacting your budget

We understand that income can fluctuate, especially with overtime or reduced hours. If you expect your **pre-tax household earnings** to be significantly higher or lower than the **$52,800** you listed, please let us know so we can update your file.

Warmly,

Janet Rosen
Program Coordinator, Brighton Food Cupboard
(585) 555-6767

metadata.subject: Documentation Needed for Holiday Assistance Application

artifacts.content:
Title: Zoom – Therapist: Budget & Anxiety Check-In
Date: 2024-09-09 4:00 PM–4:50 PM
Location: Upstairs bedroom – Zoom link in MyChart
Attendees: Carla, Dr. Weiss

Notes for session:

• Talk about constant worry over money since I stopped teaching.
• Daniel’s job at Rochester Regional Health is stable but his **salary is only about $63,000 before taxes**, plus whatever overtime they throw at him. Some months it feels like plenty; other months between co-pays, parking, and the kids’ activities, it feels like nothing.
• Mention that my little crochet side business brought in about $1,250 **gross** last year, but after yarn and booth fees it’s more like a hobby than real income.
• I keep seeing numbers on forms – Medicaid screening said our **gross household income** was “around $64k,” but the counselor at the imaging center wrote down **$61,500** when I tried to explain everything. I feel stupid that I can’t keep the exact number straight.
• Ask for help separating real financial risk from my health anxiety. I spiral when I see any form asking for “total earned annual gross household income before taxes and deductions.”
• Also talk about guilt that Lucia is thinking about colleges while we’re still trying to figure out how far Daniel’s **$63k-ish** income can stretch.

Reminder: Have notebook nearby with list of medical bills and the budget spreadsheet Daniel made.

metadata.title: Zoom – Therapist: Budget & Anxiety Check-In
metadata.location: Upstairs bedroom – Zoom link in MyChart

artifacts.content:
From: Carla J. Mendoza <c.mendoza4827@gmail.com>
To: Daniel Mendoza <dan.mendoza@rrhealth.org>
Date: January 7, 2025 8:42 PM
Subject: Drafting the financial aid form – need your eyes

Hey love,

I started filling out that hospital financial assistance application you picked up from billing. I’m stuck on the section asking for our “total earned annual gross household income before taxes and deductions.” The instructions are confusing – they want the full year’s amount from all jobs, even if they were part-time or temporary.

I pulled your last pay stub from 2024 and it shows your year-to-date gross from Rochester Regional at **$72,940.18**, but I’m not sure if that includes the on‑call overtime from November. I also added what I made from Willow Ridge Threads – I totaled all my Etsy deposits and craft fair cash and it came to about **$1,950** before yarn and booth fees. I know that’s not much, but I don’t want to leave anything out and have them accuse us of hiding income.

I still have to track down Lucia’s tiny summer babysitting money and see if that even counts. While I was digging through papers I also saw the bills from Wilmot – the hereditary cancer clinic visit with Sarah Klein was $275, and the BRCA lab part left us with a $410 balance after insurance. We’ve been paying that off in $50 monthly installments since October, so I’m hoping that plus the chemo copays will help our case.

Can you double‑check the gross number from your HR portal tomorrow so I don’t mess this up?

– C

metadata.subject: Drafting the financial aid form – need your eyes

artifacts.content:
From: Patient Financial Services <pfs@wilmot.org>
To: Carla Mendoza <c.mendoza4827@gmail.com>
Date: January 22, 2025 9:16 AM
Subject: Incomplete Financial Assistance Application

Dear Ms. Mendoza,

We are writing regarding your recently submitted application for the Wilmot Cancer Institute Patient Financial Assistance Program. After an initial review, your application is considered INCOMPLETE.

The primary missing item is documentation of your total earned annual gross household income (income before taxes and deductions) for the 2024 calendar year. The pay stubs you attached for your spouse show only partial‑year earnings (through September) with a year‑to‑date gross of **$51,433.72**, and we cannot accurately determine your full‑year household income from this alone.

In addition, the handwritten note referencing your small business income from crochet sales ("approximately **$2,000** before expenses from Willow Ridge Threads") does not include supporting documentation such as bank statements, 1099 forms, or a simple profit‑and‑loss summary.

To complete your application, please provide:

1. A copy of your spouse’s final 2024 pay stub or W‑2 from Rochester Regional Health.
2. Documentation of your 2024 self‑employment income (gross, before expenses).
3. If available, a copy of your 2023 federal tax return to help us verify your typical income range (our records show reported adjusted gross income of **$61,200** for that year).

You may upload documents via the patient portal or mail copies using the enclosed return envelope. If we do not receive the requested information within 30 days, your application will be closed.

Sincerely,

Angela Porter
Financial Counseling Specialist
Wilmot Cancer Institute
(585) 555‑3920

metadata.subject: Incomplete Financial Assistance Application

artifacts.content:
From: Elena Alvarez <e.alvarezRN@gmail.com>
To: Carla Mendoza <c.mendoza4827@gmail.com>
Date: December 29, 2024 4:03 PM
Subject: Tax stuff + that genetic bill you mentioned

Hey sis,

I finally talked to my friend who does taxes on the side. She said when you’re filling out those hospital forms and later your actual tax return, you have to keep the “total earned annual gross household income” separate from everything else, even if it feels like one big money pot.

She suggested you start a simple spreadsheet for 2024 with three columns:

1) Daniel’s gross pay from Rochester Regional (she guessed it’ll land somewhere in the low **$70,000s** once his December paycheck hits, including overtime),
2) Your crochet income – all the Willow Ridge Threads sales before yarn, booth fees, etc. (you told me you thought it was about **$1,200–$1,300**), and
3) Any side stuff like Daniel helping Miguel with that basement wiring (if he got paid for it).

She also said to keep a separate tab for medical expenses. That’s where you’d put the chemo copays, PT visits, and that hereditary cancer clinic appointment. You mentioned the genetic counselor, Sarah Klein, billed $275 and the BRCA lab portion left you with a $410 balance after insurance, and you’ve been paying it off in $50 chunks since October. Those details will matter when you’re checking if you hit the medical expense threshold.

I know it’s exhausting, but if you get this organized now, tax season won’t feel so awful. I can come down one Saturday in January and help you sort receipts if you want.

Love you,
Elena

metadata.subject: Tax stuff + that genetic bill you mentioned

artifacts.content:
From: Carla J. Mendoza <c.mendoza4827@gmail.com>
To: Sarah Klein, LCSW <sklein@strongbehavioral.org>
Date: February 27, 2025 9:14 AM
Subject: Updated contact info & financial stressors before next session

Hi Sarah,

Before our appointment next Tuesday, I wanted to send you a quick update for your chart and also give you a bit more context on the money worries we’ve been talking about.

First, my contact info: my cell is still the best way to reach me or leave reminders—(585) 442-0913. We dropped the old landline at the house on Willow Ridge Drive to cut costs, so if the office needs to confirm or reschedule, please use my cell only or MyChart.

On the financial side, things have been tighter than I admitted at first. Besides my own oncology co-pays, lymphedema therapy, and all the prescriptions, I’ve been helping my parents with my dad’s rehab bills. After his mild stroke last July, Miguel’s been doing physical and speech therapy at Rochester Regional’s outpatient rehab center, and there were balances that Medicare didn’t cover. Since August 2024 I’ve been sending about $120 every month toward those unpaid co-pays so he can keep going to his sessions.

It doesn’t sound like a huge amount, but on top of my cancer-related expenses, parking downtown, and the kids’ activities, I’m feeling pretty stretched and guilty—both if I help and if I don’t.

Could we spend a little time next session talking about how to set boundaries around this without feeling like a bad daughter?

Thank you,
Carla

metadata.subject: Updated contact info & financial stressors before next session

Hi there,

I just finished the online intake forms and realized I might have confused myself on the contact section, so I wanted to clarify before my consult next week.

The best number to reach me on weekdays is my CELL: 585-317-9048. I keep that phone nearby during the day, even during naps, and I can usually call back quickly if I miss something. Our home landline still exists technically, but we barely use it and the voicemail is unreliable, so please don’t use that for appointment reminders.

If you need a backup number in case my cell is dead or I’m at an infusion and can’t talk, my husband Daniel’s cell is 585-555-1283. He works for Rochester Regional Health and can usually step out to take a quick call. Texting either number for scheduling changes is totally fine.

Since March 2024 I’ve also been getting small amounts of extra income, usually $80–$150 a month, from selling my crocheted chemo caps and prayer shawls to other patients and families I meet during my infusion days at the Wilmot Cancer Institute at Strong Memorial Hospital. One of the nurses, Melissa, suggested I start selling the hats I was already making for the women in our Wednesday support group, so now people sometimes text me on my cell to place small orders.

Thank you,
Carla Mendoza

Rochester, NY 14618

Subject: Updated contact info for the oncology social worker

Hi Daniel,

I finally got a call back from the oncology social worker this afternoon. She said she’d been trying to reach me on the house line and my old cell number ending in 7742, so no wonder we kept missing each other. I reminded her that I barely check voicemail on the landline anymore and that I mostly use my newer cell: 585-317-8840. I also gave her your work cell in case she needs to reach you after hours about billing questions.

We talked for a bit about ways to document all the little bits of income I’ve been piecing together. I told her that in January and February 2024 I made roughly $350 selling leftover crocheted inventory on Facebook Marketplace to women in the “Rochester Breast Cancer Warriors” group after I posted about my Stage II diagnosis and the OmniSeq testing I was trying to pay for. A few of them even sent extra via Zelle “for the fight,” which still makes me tear up.

She suggested we keep a simple spreadsheet with dates, amounts, and how it was paid, in case any of it matters for financial aid or taxes. Can you help me set that up on the shared drive this weekend?

Love,
Carla

artifacts.content:
From: Carla J. Mendoza <c.mendoza4827@gmail.com>
To: Daniel Mendoza <dan.mendoza@rrh.org>
Date: 2025-01-07 09:42 AM
Subject: Insurance portal registration + money stuff

Hey Dan,

Just finished that new-patient/financial responsibility packet Dr. Kwan’s office sent over for the January 23rd follow-up. Thought I’d jot this down before chemo brain wipes it.

On the “section 2   responsible party information” page I listed you as the primary since you’re the one with the active employer coverage. They wanted full legal name *with* middle initial, so I put “Daniel R. Mendoza” and made sure the “MI responsible party” box just had the letter R. I double-checked the policy number from your last RRH card, so it should match what HR has on file.

They also had a section about “additional income.” I hesitated, but I went ahead and mentioned that since April 2024 my parents, Miguel and Rosa, have been quietly slipping me $100–$150 in cash every month after Sunday Mass at Our Lady of Guadalupe. They keep telling Father Ramirez it’s their “donation for Carla’s treatment.” I framed it as informal family support that helps us cover groceries and gas for the oncology visits.

If you log into the portal later, can you confirm everything looks right and that they didn’t switch me to out-of-network by mistake again? I really don’t want another surprise bill like the lymphedema therapy fiasco.

Also, Lucia needs the car Thursday for her practice drive to school, so I’ll try to schedule my labs early that morning. Text me your on-call schedule when you get a minute.

Love you,
Carla

metadata.subject: Insurance portal registration + money stuff

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Mendoza <daniel.mendoza@rrh.org>
To: Carla Mendoza <cjmendoza76@gmail.com>
Date: 2025-01-07 08:14 AM
Subject: Insurance form – responsible party section

Hey Car,

I just scanned the intake packet from the new GI specialist into the shared drive for you. There’s a section on page 2 called “Responsible Party Information” that confused me a bit, so I wanted to flag it before you fill it out.

They’ve got a line for the responsible party’s name with separate boxes for first name, middle initial, and last name. When I’ve done these for you in the past, I usually write “Daniel” for first, leave the middle initial blank since I don’t use one, and then “Mendoza” for last. Sometimes they pre‑print it from the insurance card, but this one looks like it needs to be handwritten or typed in.

If they really insist on a middle initial, you could just put “D” like my dad does on some of his paperwork, but honestly I’ve never had an issue leaving it empty. Just make sure they understand I’m the policyholder and the one they should bill, since your disability claim is still pending and we don’t want things bouncing back and forth.

Let me know if you want me to swing by the office with you to drop it off, or if you end up uploading it through their portal instead.

Love you,
Daniel

metadata.subject: Insurance form – responsible party section

artifacts.content:
From: Elena Alvarez <ealvarezRN@gmail.com>
To: Carla Mendoza <cjmendoza76@gmail.com>
Date: 2024-11-03 09:52 PM
Subject: Re: Question about that "responsible party" line

Hey sis,

Just saw your message about the new oncology follow‑up forms. On those “Responsible Party Information” sections, they’re basically asking who is financially responsible for the bill, not who’s emotionally responsible (if it were that, I’d put Mom, ha).

For the name fields, you should keep it exactly how it appears on the primary policyholder’s insurance card. If Daniel’s card says “Daniel A Mendoza” then use “Daniel” as the first name, “A” as the middle initial, and “Mendoza” as the last name. If the card doesn’t show a middle letter at all, I usually tell patients to write “N/A” in the middle initial box so the front desk doesn’t guess and put something random in.

The reason they’re picky is that the billing software matches the first name and the middle letter from the form to what’s on file with the insurance company. If there’s a mismatch, claims can get delayed or denied, which you absolutely don’t need on top of everything else.

So: copy the spelling from the card, including that middle letter if it’s on there. Don’t use your maiden name or Dad’s surname anywhere on that line, or it’ll just confuse them.

Call me while you’re filling it out if you want me to double‑check anything.

Love,
Elena

metadata.subject: Re: Question about that "responsible party" line

artifacts.content:
Title: Fill out UR Imaging online intake forms

Date: 2025-02-18 3:30 PM–4:15 PM
Location: Home – dining room laptop
Attendees: Carla J. Mendoza

Notes:

Log into the University of Rochester Imaging portal before Thursday’s scan. Need to complete ALL sections of the electronic intake packet so we’re not scrambling at check‑in.

To‑do while filling out forms:

- Patient info: confirm legal name as “Carla J Mendoza” (first name, middle initial, last name). Make sure it matches the driver’s license.
- Responsible Party section: they separate the boxes again (first, middle initial, last). Use the information from Daniel’s Excellus card for the responsible party name. The sample they showed in the help text used “John Q Public,” with the “Q” in the middle initial field – follow that format.
- Double‑check subscriber ID and group number from Daniel’s insurance card in the blue folder.
- Upload front and back images of the card.
- There’s a question about whether the responsible party is the same person as the patient – select “Yes” only if Daniel is listed as the patient. In this case, it’s “No,” since you’re the patient and he’s just the one the bills go to.

Set reminder: 1 hour before, so there’s time to find the insurance folder and your reading glasses.

metadata.title: Fill out UR Imaging online intake forms

artifacts.content:
From: Brighton Hematology & Oncology <frontdesk@brightonhemonc.com>
To: Carla Mendoza <cjmendoza76@gmail.com>
Date: 2024-10-09 01:11 PM
Subject: Incomplete Registration – Please Update Responsible Party Details

Dear Ms. Mendoza,

During your last visit on 10/07, our staff noticed that the “Responsible Party Information” section of your registration form was left partially incomplete. Specifically, the field for the responsible party’s middle initial was left blank, while the first and last name fields were completed.

For insurance and billing purposes, we must have the responsible party’s full legal name as it appears on the insurance policy, including any middle initial or middle name abbreviation. For example, if the insurance card lists the subscriber as “Daniel A Mendoza,” you should enter the responsible party’s name as:

First Name: Daniel
Middle Initial: A
Last Name: Mendoza

If the responsible party does not have a middle initial on the insurance card, you may enter “N/A” in that box. Please do not use nicknames or omit the middle letter if one appears on the card, as this can result in delayed or denied claims.

You may update this information in one of three ways: through the patient portal under “Profile > Responsible Party,” by calling our billing office at (585) 555‑0134, or by completing a new paper form at your next appointment.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter and for helping us keep your records accurate.

Sincerely,
Brighton Hematology & Oncology Registration Team

metadata.subject: Incomplete Registration – Please Update Responsible Party Details

The system is flagging the discrepancy because the middle initial doesn’t match, and it’s asking me to confirm which one is correct before I can finalize the update.

Can you reply and let me know which middle initial you actually use on your insurance card and billing statements? Once I have that, I’ll correct the “Responsible Party Information” section so your claims don’t get delayed again. Right now, the form has:

Responsible Party Name: Mendoza, Carla L
Relationship to Patient: Self

If the middle initial on your driver’s license or Social Security record is different from what’s on the insurance card, tell me which version your insurer prefers us to use. We just need to be consistent across all documents.

Mom,

I’m working on the FAFSA again (yay…) and I’m stuck on the “Parent/Responsible Party” section. The form is super picky about how I enter your name, including your middle initial, and it has to match what’s on the tax return and Social Security records.

When I pulled up your old Brighton School District W‑2 from the file cabinet, it shows you as **Carla J. Alvarez‑Mendoza**. But when I logged into the IRS transcript tool, it listed **Carla C Mendoza** as the primary taxpayer on the return Dad filed. And then my College Board account for the CSS Profile has **Carla J. Alvarez** as the parent responsible for payment.

Can you email me back with exactly how you want your name to appear for all this financial aid stuff? The FAFSA specifically says to use the legal name and middle initial of the parent who is the primary responsible party for the student. If what’s on your Social Security card is different from the tax return, I probably need the version that matches the tax records.

Lucia

artifacts.content:
Title: Call with billing – correct responsible party name

Notes:

Carla, remember to call Strong Memorial Hospital billing office to straighten out the “responsible party” section on your oncology account. The last statement that came in the mail listed:

Responsible Party: Mendoza, Carla M

They also had your address slightly wrong (Willow Ridge “Dr.” was missing), which might be why some of the earlier bills went to the old P.O. Box in Irondequoit that your parents used years ago.

Questions to ask during the call:
- Confirm that the account should list you as the responsible party, not Daniel.
- Make sure they have the correct spelling of your full name and the right middle initial.
- Verify that your parents’ address is removed from your profile so their mailbox doesn’t keep getting your statements.
- Ask if the incorrect middle initial on the responsible party line could affect payment posting or insurance coordination.

You wrote in your notebook that the rep you spoke with last time thought the middle initial might have been pulled from an ancient pediatric record. She said to call back once you had a quiet moment to read her the exact name on your driver’s license.

Try to do this after lunch, when you usually have a bit more energy, and before picking up Evan from soccer. Have your insurance card and ID handy.

metadata.title: Call with billing – correct responsible party name

artifacts.content:
Subject: Dental registration – middle initial mismatch

Dear Ms. Mendoza,

We’re looking forward to seeing you at your new patient visit on March 4th. While pre‑registering your information in our system, we noticed a discrepancy in the “Responsible Party” section that we’d like to resolve ahead of time.

The electronic file sent from your previous dental provider lists the responsible party for the account as **Carla T Mendoza**, with your date of birth and current Rochester address. However, the online form you submitted through our portal this week shows **Carla J Mendoza** as the person financially responsible.

Our billing software will not allow us to finalize your chart until the name and middle initial for the responsible party match across all documents. This is especially important for claims coordination with your medical insurer, given that some of your recent dental work may be related to oncology side‑effects.

Could you please reply confirming which middle initial is correct for your legal name and insurance records? If you prefer, you may bring your driver’s license and insurance card to the appointment, and we can update the responsible party information at the front desk before you are seen.

For now, your account is temporarily set up under:

Responsible Party: Mendoza, Carla T
Relationship to Patient: Self

If we do not hear from you before your visit, we will simply verify this with you in person.

Warm regards,

Angela Russo
New Patient Coordinator
Brighton Family Dental Care

P.S. If anyone else (for example, your husband) should be listed as the responsible party instead, let us know so we can adjust the paperwork accordingly.

Dear Carla Mendoza,

Our records indicate that you recently began, but did not complete, the online New Patient Registration form for Brighton Family Practice. In the "Section 1 – Patient Information" portion, the field for "MI" (middle initial) was left blank.

For our electronic records, we prefer to have a middle initial on file whenever possible. If you do not have a legal middle name, you may choose to:

1. Leave the field blank, OR
2. Use an initial that matches any existing medical records (for example, some patients choose the first letter of a family name).

Currently, your imported chart from Rochester Regional lists you as "Carla M Mendoza," while a transferred lab result lists you as "Carla L Mendoza." To avoid delays in processing prescriptions and referrals, please respond to this email indicating which middle initial you would like us to use going forward, or confirm that you wish to have no middle initial recorded.

Please also review the employment/income section of the form. If you have any additional income (for example, side work, tutoring, crafting sales, etc.), you may include a brief description, though this is not required.

Once we receive your response, we will finalize your registration.

Sincerely,

Office Staff
Brighton Family Practice
2200 Monroe Ave, Rochester, NY 14618

Date: 2024-12-18 09:02 AM
Subject: Incomplete Demographic Information – Action Needed

Dear Carla Mendoza,

During a recent audit of our electronic health record system, we noticed that portions of your demographic profile are incomplete or inconsistent. To ensure accurate billing and communication with your insurance carrier, we need you to review and update your information.

Specifically, the following fields require attention:

• Legal name formatting (first name, middle initial, last name)
• Marital status and emergency contact
• Preferred pharmacy

Our records currently show your name in several different formats, including entries with no middle initial and entries with a middle initial that does not match what appears on your insurance card. This can lead to claim delays or denials.

When you reach the line labeled “MI,” enter the middle initial that appears on your government-issued ID and primary insurance card. metadata.subject: Upcoming Imaging Appointment – Ordering Provider Confirmation Needed

artifacts.content:
From: referrals@elizabethwende.com
To: Carla Mendoza <cjmendoza4827@email.com>
Date: 2024-10-02 3:41 PM
Subject: Referral Received from Primary Care – Action Required

Dear Ms. Mendoza,

Elizabeth Wende Breast Care has received a **screening mammogram referral** on your behalf from the following ordering physician practice:

    Dr. Jonathan P. Heller
    Highland Family Medicine

Before we can schedule your appointment, we must verify that this is the practice that should be listed as the ordering provider for your upcoming exam. If your breast imaging is now being coordinated through a different oncology or surgical practice, please contact that office and ask them to submit a new order directly to our referral fax line.

If Dr. Heller at Highland Family Medicine should remain your ordering physician for this study, please reply via the secure portal or call our scheduling team at (585) 555-2870 within 7 business days. If we do not hear from you, the referral may be closed and a new order will be required.

Please note: Our records indicate you have a history of right-sided breast cancer and prior chemotherapy. Because of this, you may be better served by diagnostic imaging coordinated through your current cancer care team rather than a routine screening study ordered by your primary care practice.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,
Referral Coordination Team
Elizabeth Wende Breast Care

artifacts.content:
From: imaging-coord@rrh.org
To: Carla Mendoza <cjmendoza4827@email.com>
Date: 2025-02-03 1:22 PM
Subject: Clarification Needed – Ordering Provider for CT Chest

Dear Ms. Mendoza,

You are currently scheduled for a **CT scan of the chest with contrast** on **February 17, 2025 at 8:30 AM** at Rochester Regional Health – Linden Oaks Imaging.

The electronic order we received lists the following as the ordering physician practice:

    Pluta Cancer Center – Medical Oncology
    Attn: Dr. Steven L. Kaplan

However, your insurance (Excellus BCBS) shows a different oncology group as your primary cancer care provider. To avoid potential claim denials, we must confirm that Pluta Cancer Center – Medical Oncology is the correct ordering practice for this exam.

Please take one of the following steps within 5 business days:
1. Call Pluta Cancer Center and ask them to verify that Dr. Kaplan is the ordering physician for this CT chest, OR
2. Contact your current oncology office and request that they submit a new CT chest order directly to Rochester Regional Health – Linden Oaks Imaging, with their practice clearly listed as the ordering provider.

If we do not receive updated confirmation, your appointment may need to be rescheduled.

If you have questions about prep (NPO after midnight, contrast allergies, etc.), please call our nurse navigator line at (585) 555-9024.

Sincerely,
Imaging Coordination Team
Rochester Regional Health

artifacts.content:
Title: PCP Follow-Up – Labs & Medication Review
Date: 2024-07-29 3:00 PM–3:40 PM
Location: Brighton Family Medicine, 2150 Monroe Ave, Rochester, NY
Attendees: Carla J. Mendoza; Dr. Karen L. Morales (Brighton Family Medicine)

Description:
Routine follow-up with primary care. Need to go over:
- Blood pressure readings from home.
- Reflux meds and sleep issues.
- Ongoing fatigue and how much might still be from chemo.

From the appointment reminder letter:

"For this visit, your primary care physician will review results ordered through our practice. Our records currently show:

    Ordering physician practice for recent lab panel:
        Dr. Karen L. Morales
        Brighton Family Medicine

If you have had labs or imaging ordered by other practices (for example, breast surgeon, radiation oncology group, or a separate medical oncology practice), please bring copies of those reports or ensure they are sent to Brighton Family Medicine prior to your appointment. This helps us coordinate your care but does not change the ordering provider for those tests."

My notes:
- Bring list of all current meds and supplements.
- Ask if she thinks I should keep having routine labs through her office or if they should all go through the cancer center instead.
- Mention that bills are getting confusing with different ordering physician practices on each statement.

Parking reminder: lot behind building gets full after 2:30 PM – leave house by 2:20.

metadata.title: PCP Follow-Up – Labs & Medication Review
attendees: Dr. Karen L. Morales (Brighton Family Medicine)
metadata.location: Brighton Family Medicine, 2150 Monroe Ave, Rochester, NY

artifacts.content:
Subject: Referral paperwork for imaging & PT

Hi Carla,

This is just a quick follow-up to our visit yesterday.

I’ve submitted the electronic referral for your follow-up breast MRI and the additional ultrasound that we discussed. Those orders will show under my practice name, **Strong Memorial Breast Health & Survivorship Clinic**, so if any of the scheduling staff ask who the ordering physician/practice is, you can just tell them it came from the Breast Health & Survivorship Clinic at Strong.

Separately, I’ve renewed your referral for lymphedema therapy so you can continue with your arm exercises. You mentioned you’re also doing pelvic floor physical therapy at **Lattimore Physical Therapy in Brighton** with **Jenna Morris** for chemo-related incontinence issues. Since she’s considered out-of-network on your plan, our system doesn’t generate those orders the same way, but you can still list my name and the Strong Memorial Breast Health & Survivorship Clinic as the referring practice on any forms they give you.

If the imaging center or PT office needs clarification, they can fax us at 585-555-0132 or call the clinic directly. Just make sure you keep copies of any receipts, especially since you said you’re paying about $95 per session and usually going twice a month.

Let me know via MyChart if you run into any trouble with scheduling.

Best,

Karen Patel, MD
Strong Memorial Breast Health & Survivorship Clinic
Department of Surgery, UR Medicine

artifacts.content:
Subject: Re: Clarifying referral source for pelvic PT

Hi Jenna,

Thanks for checking in about the paperwork. I called Excellus this morning, and they told me that for your claims, the ordering physician practice they have listed is **UR Medicine Women’s Health – Highland Women’s Center**, under **Dr. Melissa Grant**. That’s from when she first wrote the script in January 2024 for pelvic floor physical therapy after my chemo.

Even though I’m being seen for breast cancer follow-up elsewhere now, they said they don’t need a new order as long as Dr. Grant’s practice stays on the referral. The frustrating part is that you’re still considered out-of-network, so after their limited reimbursement, I’m paying about $95 per session out-of-pocket. Since we’re doing this twice a month, it’s adding roughly $190 every month to our medical expenses on top of the oncology co-pays.

If you need to update anything on your end, the insurance rep said you should continue to list **UR Medicine Women’s Health – Highland Women’s Center** as the ordering/referring practice on the claim forms. They stressed that it needs to match exactly what’s in their system.

Let me know if you want me to bring a copy of the original referral to my next appointment. I have it in my big medical binder along with the lymphedema PT orders.

Thanks again for all your help and for being so patient with my million questions.

Best,
Carla

Carla J. Mendoza
4827 Willow Ridge Drive
Rochester, NY 14618

metadata.subject: Re: Clarifying referral source for pelvic PT

artifacts.content:
Title: Follow-up with surgical oncologist – Highland Breast Center

Description:

Six-month surgical follow-up at **Highland Breast Center**.

From reminder letter:
- Physician: **Dr. Laura McKenna**
- Ordering physician practice on record for this visit: **Highland Breast Center – UR Medicine Surgical Oncology**
- Reason: Post-lumpectomy exam, review of imaging, scar and lymphedema check.

Prep notes:
- Bring latest imaging CD from Strong.
- Ask whether future mammograms and ultrasounds should still be ordered through Highland Breast Center – UR Medicine Surgical Oncology, or if they prefer I route them through medical oncology instead.
- Bring list of questions about tightness in right arm and tingling in fingers.

Insurance/expenses:
- Mention that I’m already stretched thin with co-pays and out-of-pocket costs.
- Specifically bring up pelvic floor PT at **Lattimore Physical Therapy in Brighton** with **Jenna Morris**. Because she’s out-of-network, I’m paying about $95 per session, usually twice a month (~$190/month). Ask if there is an in-network pelvic PT they recommend or if they can help with a medical necessity letter to support appeals.

Logistics:
- Location: Highland Breast Center, 1000 South Ave, Rochester, NY
- Parking: South Garage – remember to bring ticket for validation.
- Plan to leave house by 8:15 a.m. to allow time for traffic and check-in.

artifacts.content:
Subject: Insurance form – need exact wording for ordering practice

Hi Daniel,

Can you take a look at the attached Excellus form when you get a breather? They kicked back the claim for my January MRI because the “ordering physician practice” line didn’t match what they have in their system.

I had written “UR Cancer Center – Breast Clinic,” but when I called, the rep said it has to be exactly **“UR Medicine Comprehensive Breast Care at Pluta”** with **Dr. Henry Collins** listed as the ordering physician. Apparently the imaging center submitted it that way originally, and anything else confuses their software.

The form is asking me to confirm the ordering practice and sign. Before I send it back, I just want to be sure I’m copying the name correctly from the explanation of benefits. Can you log into the insurance portal and double-check that the ordering physician practice for that MRI is indeed “UR Medicine Comprehensive Breast Care at Pluta” and not the general UR Medicine Oncology department?

While you’re in there, can you also see if the pelvic floor PT claims from **Lattimore Physical Therapy in Brighton** are showing up? Those are the ones with **Jenna Morris** that we’re paying about $95 per session for, twice a month. I’m trying to figure out how much of that they’re actually reimbursing.

Sorry to dump this on you after a long shift. I just don’t want to mess up the wording and delay the reimbursement again.

Love,
Carla

metadata.subject: Insurance form – need exact wording for ordering practice
